text: Welcome to this video of a robotic right upper lobe lobectomy with chest wall resection, performed by Vijay Joshi and Joel Dunning and edited by Jason Trevis. We present the case of a seventy five year old female with a highly PET positive peripherally located tumour. As a result, the plan was to perform a wed resection of the tumour and send for frozen section, with scope to perform a full chest wall resection if there were inadequate margins. We begin with the staple firings for the wedge resection of the tumour. The stapler in use being that it's produced by Lexington Medical, which makes staplers that can fit the Covidian gun, but at a thirty percent less cost. We move to the final firing of the wedge resection. And now beginning the resection of the chest wall, aiming for good margins as we dissect of the second and third ribs with the spatula in arm one and cadia forceps in arm two.

The surgeon completes the wedge resection and proceeds with the right upper lobe lobectomy. A Cavidian Tan stapler is used to staple the right upper lobe vein and the truncal branch of the pulmonary artery. Dissection of the subcarinal station seven lymph node is performed, followed by removal of the station R-ten lymph node located in front of the pulmonary artery and below the azygos vein. A sling is placed around the right upper lobe bronchus, and a curve tip stapler is used for the final firing. The posterior ascending branch of the pulmonary artery is visible. The surgeon completes the arterial and bronchial firings and moves to the fissure between the upper and middle lobe, completing the firings from posteriorly to anteriorly. The lobe is placed into a bag and removed.

The surgeon uses a jiggly saw with robotic arms to cut through the third rib posteriorly, followed by the second rib. An axillary incision is utilized to cut the ribs anteriorly with a rib cutter. The ribs are then dissected off the muscles of the anterior chest wall, with assistance pushing the ribs to facilitate cutting in the correct plane.

The surgeon completes the dissection of the second and third ribs positioned under the scapula, using robotic ports and an auxiliary incision. The external view shows the placement of these ports and incision.
